sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.80 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group, and$$an irreducible complex character of$G$. We call$$a$$-character if$$is a constituent of$(1_H)^G$for some maximal subgroup$H$of~$G$; and$$is said to be monomial if$$is induced by a linear character of a subgroup of$G$. :$G$is solvable if and only if all its$$-characters are monomial. The necessity part of the conjecture is true (G.\,Qian, Y.\,Yang, , 46, no.\,1 (2018), ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
